channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nvidia expects ban on chip exports to China to cost $5.5B
item.description: Nvidia now expects new US government restrictions on exports of its H20 chip to China will cost the company as much as $5.5 billion, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Docusign expands beyond signatures with new AI-powered contract management tools
item.description: For about 20 years, Docusign has been known as a tool for collecting digital signatures—helping businesses replace paper forms with electronic versions that are just as secure and legally binding.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Tatooine-like planet outside the solar system may orbit two failed stars, scientists say
item.description: Scientists may have found a new Tatooine-like planet outside the solar system that orbits two failed stars.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Toshiba's wireless portable turntable is the Sound Burger of 2025 – just don't take it to beach parties
item.description: Toshiba's unveiled a new portable vinyl record player set to be shipped this month, but while it looks like a lot of fun, serious vinyl collectors may be a little wary.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Surfshark finally adds split tunneling on macOS – making it one of the few VPNs to have it
item.description: Surfshark Bypasser finally lands on macOS, but it's also available on Windows, Android, iOS, and a browser extension. Here's how you should use it.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: The Los Angeles Times' Newfound Push for Viewpoint Diversity
item.description: The Los Angeles Times' shift towards viewpoint diversity has raised concerns among critics, with some arguing that the publisher is trying to appease President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hertz says hackers stole customer data in vendor breach
item.description: Hertz is notifying customers that hackers may have stolen personal information including credit card details and Social Security numbers during a data breach on one of its vendors.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Zuckerberg suggested spinning Instagram off into separate company
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg previously considered spinning off Instagram into a separate company in the face of growing antitrust concerns and competing interests with Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Underwater robots, great white sharks and glowing jellyfish: New $50 million high-tech ship arrives to unlock ocean mysteries
item.description: The 'David Packard' is named for the Silicon Valley pioneer who donated more than $1 billion to ocean research.
item.dc:creator: Paul Rogers, The Mercury News

item.title: JRuby 10 brings faster startup times
item.description: JRuby 10, the latest release of the Ruby language variant built atop the JVM, has arrived, bringing startup time improvements, support for Java 21, and compatibility with Ruby 3.4. Release of JRuby 10 was announced April 14. JRuby 10 can be downloaded from jruby.org.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The VA EHR modernization roadmap under the Trump administration
item.description: A key focus will be on modernizing federal technology and software to maximize governmental efficiency and productivity. The post The VA EHR modernization roadmap under the Trump administration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg spars with FTC lawyer over meaning of emails cited in antitrust trial
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg sparred Tuesday with a Federal Trade Commission attorney over the meaning of emails the Facebook founder wrote about why he wanted to acquire Instagram, as a historic antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market went into its second day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Lego just dropped 9 new sets, but as a Star Wars obsessed fan, I'd shop these three first
item.description: Lego is celebrating with 'May the 4th' early with nine new sets and plenty of freebies, but these are three to shop.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: AI’s new ally: Why synthetic data matters for government AI adoption
item.description: The term “synthetic data” refers to artificially-generated data that mimics the patterns and properties of real-world datasets. The post AI’s new ally: Why synthetic data matters for government AI adoption first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hello, Flipper! Google has developed a genAI model to talk to dolphins
item.description: Google has unveiled a new generative AI (genAI) model, Dolphin Gemma, which will attempt to decode how dolphins communicate with each other. Dolphins are among the most intelligent animals on the planet and communicate partly through whistles and calls.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DDoS-protection crisis looms as attacks grow
item.description: Every year, distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks break records for frequency, size, and sophistication, making it imperative that enterprises adopt powerful mitigation measures.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple’s big plan for better AI is you
item.description: Apple has a brand new plan to make Apple Intelligence smarter. That plan is you — more specifically, your data. A post on the company’s Machine Learning blog explains the plan to begin on-device analysis of your user data.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 10 security flaws leave millions vulnerable
item.description: Microsoft patches 12 critical flaws, but six have already been exploited by criminals. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says updating your system is only a short-term fix.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intelligence at the edge opens up more risks: how unified SASE can solve it
item.description: In an increasingly mobile and modern workforce, smart technologies such as AI-driven edge solutions and the Internet of Things (IoT) can help enterprises improve productivity and efficiency—whether to address operational roadblocks or respond faster to market demands.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Keysight tools tackle data center deployment efficiency
item.description: Test and performance measurement vendor Keysight Technologies has developed Keysight Artificial Intelligence (KAI) to identify performance inhibitors affecting large GPU deployments. It emulates workload profiles, rather than using actual resources, to pinpoint performance bottlenecks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI humanoid robot learns to mimic human emotions and behavior
item.description: A groundbreaking robot that's like a real-life Wall-E uses advanced artificial intelligence to replicate natural gestures and deliberate actions with striking accuracy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Via porn, gore and ultra-violence, extremist groups are sinking hooks online into the very young
item.description: PARIS (AP) — After his arrest, the boy's mother was stunned to discover that her 12-year-old had been learning how to kill and gorging on videos of decapitation and torture so gruesome they made even case-hardened French court officials look away.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hackers find a way around built-in Windows protections
item.description: If not properly managed, Windows Defender Application Control, a security feature with Windows, could be a potential security vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: White House record-keeping revamp
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHS employees consider workforce transition amid major uncertainty
item.description: Staff across DHS, including at CISA and FEMA, are considering deferred resignation or other offers amid reports that their agencies will be targeted for cuts. The post DHS employees consider workforce transition amid major uncertainty first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Can't find your Android phone? Here's what to do to track it down
item.description: There are multiple ways to find your missing Android phone after it goes missing. One method involves using a smartwatch to ping your phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What is data fabric? How it offers a unified view of your data
item.description: What is data fabric? Data fabric is a type of architecture that aims to provide unified access to the data stored in various places across your organization.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: More and faster: New proposals changing Python from within
item.description: Top picks for Python readers on InfoWorld Making Python faster won’t be easy, but it’ll be worth it Python’s malleability and dynamism make it powerful, but also very hard to speed up. Here’s a look at some of the ways developers are working to make Python faster from within.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Arm backs big data center ambitions with migration tools
item.description: Arm Holdings expects its architecture will account for half of the data center CPU market share by the end of this year, and to help achieve those goals it has launched a batch of new migration tools for application porting.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google unveils Firebase Studio for AI app development
item.description: Google is previewing Firebase Studio, a cloud-based agentic development environment designed to build, test, deploy, and run AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World
